string.description: Text value, double-quoted allows variable expansion and escapes, single-quoted is literal

integer.description: Whole number used in arithmetic via $(( )) and test comparisons

array.description: Indexed array of strings declared with parentheses, accessed with ${arr[i]}

exit_status.description: Integer 0-255 returned by commands; 0 means success, non-zero is failure

file_descriptor.description: Integer handle for I/O streams used with redirection operators > < 2>

pid.description: Process ID number for the current shell or background processes

glob_pattern.description: Filename expansion pattern with wildcards for matching multiple paths

indexed_array.description: Ordered array indexed by non-negative integers starting at 0. Can be sparse. Created implicitly with name=() or name[n]=value, or explicitly with declare -a.

associative_array.description: Key-value map using arbitrary string keys. Must be explicitly declared with declare -A. Available since Bash 4.0. Cannot be exported.

nameref.description: Name reference variable that acts as an alias for another variable. All operations on the nameref affect the referenced variable. Available since Bash 4.3. Useful for passing variable names to functions.

readonly.description: Immutable variable that cannot be reassigned or unset after declaration. Applies to strings, integers, and arrays.

exported.description: Variable marked for export to child processes. The value is copied into the environment of subsequently executed commands.

lowercase.description: Variable with lowercase attribute. Any value assigned is automatically converted to lowercase.

uppercase.description: Variable with uppercase attribute. Any value assigned is automatically converted to uppercase.

function.signature: name() { commands; }  or  function name { commands; }
function.description: Shell function. Takes positional parameters ($1, $2, ...), can declare local variables, and returns an exit status (0-255) via return.

regex.description: POSIX extended regular expression used with the =~ operator in [[ ]]. Capture groups populate BASH_REMATCH array. Do not quote the regex pattern.

float.description: Floating-point variable type declared with typeset -F or float builtin

glob.description: Extended glob patterns with qualifiers for recursive matching and file filtering
